answers.answer: Mock `@/lib/query/hooks` at the module level. This is the dominant pattern in the codebase: 7 test files (VariableRow, ConditionalEditor, AssumptionPanel, EventTradePanel, ResolveMarketPanel, TradingPanel, DiscussionThread) all mock hooks directly with `vi.mock('@/lib/query/hooks', () => ({ hookName: vi.fn() }))`. Only API-level tests (MarketDetail, MarketList, Portfolio, Analytics) mock `@/lib/api/client`. Since BayesNetGraph uses 3 hooks (useMarkets, useMarket, useEngineStats) and NodeWithDetail calls useMarket per-node, mocking hooks directly avoids N async queries, eliminates the need for QueryClient wrapping and waitFor, and matches the established pattern for component tests that consume hooks.
answers.uncertainty: None — the pattern is clearly established and appropriate for this component.

answers.answer: Use `screen.getByText()` for SVG text content — it works with SVG <text> elements in jsdom and is already used throughout the codebase. PriceChart.test.tsx uses getByText for y-axis probability labels and legend names (lines 105-109, 167-168). VolumeChart.test.tsx uses getByText for trade count labels and y-axis currency labels (lines 67-68, 109-111). For structural SVG queries (counting rect/circle/line elements, checking attributes like stroke-width), use `container.querySelectorAll()` as PriceChart and VolumeChart tests do. Do NOT add data-testid attributes to the component — while PriceChart.tsx and VolumeChart.tsx have them, the task explicitly says not to modify the component source.
answers.uncertainty: If the same text appears in multiple SVG text elements (e.g., identical outcome names across nodes), getAllByText + length assertion or container.querySelectorAll('text') may be needed.

answers.answer: Use a single generic mockReturnValue for most tests, and switch to mockImplementation with per-ID data only for the probability bars test. For most tests (loading, empty state, node rendering, edges, cliques, legend, stats bar), a single return value applied to all useMarket calls is sufficient — the tests care about structure, not per-node data differences. For the probability bars test (test case 11), use `mockImplementation((id) => ...)` to return specific outcomes and marginals per market ID so assertions can verify exact bar content. This matches ConditionalEditor.test.tsx which uses a single mockReturnValue for useMarket (line 160-164), and VariableRow.test.tsx which also uses a single return value (line 154-160).
answers.uncertainty: If tests assert on specific node titles alongside probability bars, the mock may need per-ID switching more broadly. But the component gets titles from useMarkets (via layoutNodes), not useMarket, so this shouldn't be an issue.

answers.answer: Use the real formatProbability implementation — do NOT mock it. It is a pure function with no side effects: `(p * 100).toFixed(1) + '%'` (format.ts:1-3). Mocking it would add complexity for zero benefit. Using the real function means test assertions match exact rendered output (e.g., `'75.0%'` for p=0.75), which is more faithful. PriceChart.test.tsx already uses the real formatProbability when asserting on y-axis labels (lines 105-109), confirming this is the established pattern.

Test both: (1) the stats bar clique count text AND (2) the CliqueOverlay rendering/non-rendering edge case. The stats bar displays `cliques.length` from engineStats regardless of whether each clique has 2+ members (BayesNetGraph.tsx:275). But CliqueOverlay silently drops cliques with < 2 member nodes (line 179: `if (memberNodes.length < 2) return null`). This means the stats bar could say '2 cliques' while only 1 renders in SVG. Testing both behaviors is important for coverage: provide engineStats with 2 cliques where one has only 1 node, verify the stats bar says '2 cliques', but only 1 'Clique X' text label appears in the SVG. This is a meaningful behavioral distinction worth testing explicitly.
answers.uncertainty: Whether the task's test cases already cover this edge case or expect it as a separate test. The analysis should make this a dedicated test case rather than assuming it's covered elsewhere.

assumption_checks.impact: This is the right approach. 7 test files follow this exact pattern. The vi.mock factory + vi.mocked + mockReturnValue pattern is well-established.
Confirmed: PriceChart.test.tsx and VolumeChart.test.tsx both use screen.getByText() successfully for SVG <text> element content. No need for data-testid on the component.
formatProbability is a 1-line pure function. Real implementation is already used in PriceChart tests. No mock needed.
File location is explicitly specified in the task. No ambiguity.

Single mockReturnValue works for most tests. Node titles come from useMarkets (via layoutNodes), not useMarket, so per-ID switching is only needed when asserting specific probability bar content. Use mockImplementation only in the probability bars test.

No component modifications needed. container.querySelector/querySelectorAll on SVG attributes (strokeWidth, fill, etc.) is the established pattern in PriceChart.test.tsx and VolumeChart.test.tsx. Focus node stroke-width can be verified via container.querySelector('rect[stroke-width]') or similar.

Legend items are <span> elements inside a <div> (BayesNetGraph.tsx:305-326). getByText('Active'), getByText('Resolved'), etc. will work normally. The conditional legend items ('Conditional dependency', 'Junction tree clique') only render when edges/cliques exist, which is testable via queryByText.
